commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Needs contributor real behavior proof plus a maintainer-reviewed fix to the hook default policy boundary before merge; this is not safe to route as an automated merge.; Needs attention: The diff weakens the workspace hook opt-in boundary by letting HOOK.md metadata override the source-level default enable mode.; Review finding: [P1] Keep workspace hooks explicitly opt-in ‚Äî src/hooks/policy.ts:96-97 (sha=a51b3a79eadfce4f437d0ac22c97b64789f78fbb)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a `session:aborted` internal hook event from stuck-session recovery, a bundled opt-in `auto-continue` hook, and a `defaultEnableMode` hook metadata override.

PR surface: Source +104, Tests +33, Docs +34. Total +171 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was established in this review. Source inspection shows current main returns an aborted recovery outcome without emitting a hook event, but the live stuck-session scenario was not rerun here.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor real behavior proof plus a maintainer-reviewed fix to the hook default policy boundary before merge; this is not safe to route as an automated merge.

target.title: feat(hooks): emit session:aborted + opt-in auto-continue hook
target.body:
## Summary

When the stuck-session watchdog hard-aborts an embedded run, the run goes silent ‚Äî all queued progress is lost and the agent never resumes on its own. This adds a self-healing backstop:

1. **Emit `session:aborted`** ‚Äî when `recoverStuckDiagnosticSession` actually aborts a run, it now fires an internal `session:aborted` hook event with the session id, age, lane, and `reason: "stuck_recovery"`.

2. **Opt-in `auto-continue` bundled hook** ‚Äî on `session:aborted`, re-injects a short continuation instruction and kicks a fresh turn so the agent resumes its work loop instead of going dark. A loop-guard caps resumes at 3 per session per 30 min so a pathological abort‚Üícontinue‚Üíabort cycle can't run away.

Because resuming a run automatically is an opinionated behavior change, the hook is **disabled by default**. To let a bundled hook ship disabled-by-default without abusing the workspace policy, this also adds a small declarative `defaultEnableMode` metadata field that a hook's frontmatter can set to `explicit-opt-in`. `policy.ts` honors it. Enable with:

    openclaw hooks enable auto-continue

This is purely a backstop behind the existing false-abort *prevention* (working runs already refresh the watchdog activity timestamp on every run event via the ACP projector's `onProgress`). Auto-continue only matters for a genuine hang.

## Real behavior proof

Running on a live OpenClaw 2026.5.20 gateway (systemd user service) across multiple agents:

- `openclaw hooks enable auto-continue` ‚Üí `‚úì Enabled`; hook shows up in `openclaw hooks list` as enabled.
- Before the fix: a genuinely stuck embedded run was aborted by the watchdog at ~367s and went silent ‚Äî no further output reached the chat channel.
- After the fix: the same abort now fires `session:aborted`; the hook re-injects the continuation and the agent picks its work loop back up on its own. The loop-guard correctly stops after 3 resumes inside the 30-min window.

## Test plan

- [x] `tsc -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit` ‚Äî no new errors
- [x] `vitest run src/hooks/policy.test.ts` ‚Äî 9/9 pass (incl. 2 new: bundled default-on, and opt-in disabled-by-default + enabled-when-config)
- [x] `oxlint` / `oxfmt` clean
- [x] `node scripts/build-all.mjs` ‚Äî exit 0; HOOK.md copied to dist/bundled

Allow edits by maintainers: yes.
target.author: petr1t
